name: Yokota Air Base
tagline: Headquarters of U.S. Forces Japan and the 374th Airlift Wing, on the Kanto Plain near Tokyo.
_note: OCONUS (Japan). US-only feeds intentionally omitted (overseas uses OHA + COLA, not BAH; no RentCast or SchoolDigger). Weather via the Open-Meteo global fallback. Facts verified from official sources (yokota.af.mil, usfj.mil, dodea.edu/pacific, TRICARE Overseas, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Location: Fussa, Japan (about 28 miles NW of Tokyo)
data.Also hosts: Headquarters U.S. Forces Japan and Fifth Air Force
data.Role: Strategic airlift hub for the western Pacific
data.Region: OCONUS (overseas tour; OHA and COLA, not BAH)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Yokota Air Base sits on the Kanto Plain near Tokyo and is the headquarters of U.S. Forces Japan and Fifth Air Force. The host 374th Airlift Wing flies tactical airlift and aeromedical evacuation and serves as a key strategic airlift hub for the western Pacific.
With its command headquarters and Tokyo-area location, Yokota is one of the most visible U.S. installations in Japan and a common gateway for personnel moving through the theater.

sections.title: A Tokyo-Area Tour: Cost, Sponsorship, and Living Off Base

data.subtitle: Yokota puts you near one of the world's great cities. Command sponsorship and the on-base versus off-base choice shape the tour, and Japan's allowances (OHA plus COLA) help with a high cost of living.
intro: As with all overseas tours, command sponsorship determines whether your family comes at government expense and gets housing, schools, and full benefits. Confirm it early through your gaining command.

On-base housing is convenient and in demand; off-base, you rent on the Japanese economy with OHA, plus an overseas cost-of-living allowance (COLA) that helps with the expensive Tokyo region. Living off base is an immersive experience with a different rental system and language to navigate.

recommendations.advice: Command sponsorship is what brings your family and unlocks housing, DoDEA schools, and benefits; sort it through your unit early.
On-base housing keeps you close to schools and amenities and removes the language barrier from daily life; demand is high, so get on the list early.
Renting in the towns around Fussa offers a fuller Japanese experience; OHA offsets rent and utilities up to a ceiling, and COLA helps with everyday costs.
Tokyo, Mount Fuji, and Japan's famous rail network are all close; many families say exploring Japan is the highlight of the tour.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access and in-processing are handled through the installation, and overseas access rules differ from stateside bases. You will need status documents under the U.S.-Japan SOFA.
Confirm the current visitor and access requirements on the official Yokota site before you arrive.

sections.title: Housing, OHA & COLA
data: Families choose between on-base housing and renting on the Japanese economy around Fussa. On-base housing is convenient and in high demand.

Overseas, the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) replaces stateside BAH for off-base rentals, individualized by rank, dependents, and actual rent up to a ceiling. An overseas cost-of-living allowance (COLA) also helps offset the high cost of the Tokyo region. The Housing Office assigns on-base housing and helps with off-base rentals.

sections.title: Schools
data: Children attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools rather than local districts. DoDEA Pacific operates elementary, middle, and high schools at Yokota.
Start DoDEA registration early and confirm the specific schools, boundaries, and required records on the DoDEA site as you plan your move.

data.Care: Outpatient care on base; inpatient and specialty care referred to U.S. Naval Hospital Yokosuka or host-nation facilities

intro: An overseas PCS has extra steps beyond a stateside move. Start these early, ideally before you leave.

steps.title: Confirm command sponsorship
steps.text: An accompanied (command-sponsored) tour is what lets your family come at government expense and access housing, schools, and the commissary. Confirm it through your gaining command.

steps.title: Get no-fee passports and SOFA documents
steps.text: Apply for no-fee passports for family members and the U.S.-Japan SOFA documents that let you live in-country. Tourist passports are separate.

steps.title: Line up lodging and a sponsor
steps.text: Arrange on-base temporary lodging and connect with your unit sponsor before you fly. You can claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA).

steps.title: Plan household goods and your vehicle
steps.text: Overseas shipments go by sea with weight limits, and importing a vehicle to Japan involves inspections (JCI) and costs. Many families buy a local vehicle instead; confirm the rules.

steps.title: Register the kids with DoDEA
steps.text: Children attend DoDEA Pacific schools; start registration early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up TRICARE Overseas
steps.text: Enroll in TRICARE Overseas and get a primary care manager at the 374th Medical Group clinic.

data.q: Do I get BAH at Yokota?
data.a: No. Overseas you receive OHA instead of BAH for off-base rentals, plus an overseas cost-of-living allowance (COLA) for the expensive Tokyo region. On-base housing is government-provided.
It's what brings your family at government expense and unlocks on-base housing, DoDEA schools, and benefits. Confirm it before making plans.
Often it's easier to buy a vehicle locally. Importing a car to Japan involves inspections (JCI) and costs, so weigh shipping against buying once you arrive.
A lot. Tokyo is close, with Mount Fuji, Hakone, and Japan's rail network within easy reach. Many families call exploring Japan the best part of the tour.

sections.title: Food & Things Nearby
sections.note: Live nearby places via Google Places. Tap 'Use my location' to re-center on where you are.

picks.name: Tokyo (Shibuya)
picks.blurb: The heart of the world's largest metro area, about an hour away by train.

picks.name: Mount Fuji
picks.blurb: Japan's iconic peak, with the Fuji Five Lakes region for hiking and views.

picks.name: Showa Kinen Park
picks.blurb: A huge national garden park in nearby Tachikawa, great for families.

picks.name: Hakone
picks.blurb: A hot-spring resort area with views of Mount Fuji, southwest of Tokyo.
